babyDevelopment: The baby is now able to turn its head from side to side. Nerve connections in the brain are becoming more complex. The baby is gaining weight quickly as its layers of fat continue to build. The lungs are continuing to mature.
bodyChanges: You may experience 'shortness of breath' more easily as the baby takes up more space. Pelvic pressure and backaches may intensify. You might notice 'leaking' from your breasts becoming more frequent. Sleep may be more difficult due to discomfort.
tips: Practice your breathing techniques for labor
Monitor baby's movement several times a day
Sleep with adequate support for your back and bump

do: Maintain a consistent, light exercise routine
dont: Lift heavy objects that strain your body
Ignore signs of severe, persistent back pain

travelAdvisory.note: Keep travel local; consult your doctor for any trips.

warningSignsToWatch: Sudden swelling in face or hands
milestoneDescription: Baby can now turn its head from side to side as muscles and coordination improve.
